latest_coverage_summary: Queried all nine Tier-1 government jurisdictions (AusTender, buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders/supply.qld, Tenders WA/SA/Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders) via web search; sampled Tier-2 water corporations (Sydney/Melbourne/SA Water, Power & Water NT), councils (VendorPanel public tenders, Inner West) and universities; and covered Tier-3 corporate channels (SAP Ariba Discovery, BHP, Rio Tinto Buy Local, Fortescue, NBN Co, Australia Post, Snowy Hydro). Captured one genuinely new opportunity (UNSW enterprise environmental monitoring EOI) and two new buyer-originated sources (Power & Water NT opportunities page; UNSW E-Tendering portal). As in recent runs, direct page fetching was unavailable (every external portal returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool), so detail is limited to publicly-searchable headline level and no corporate RFx could be read behind registration walls.

latest_run_metadata.coverage_summary: Queried all nine Tier-1 government jurisdictions (AusTender, buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders/supply.qld, Tenders WA/SA/Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders) via web search; sampled Tier-2 water corporations (Sydney/Melbourne/SA Water, Power & Water NT), councils (VendorPanel public tenders, Inner West) and universities; and covered Tier-3 corporate channels (SAP Ariba Discovery, BHP, Rio Tinto Buy Local, Fortescue, NBN Co, Australia Post, Snowy Hydro). Captured one genuinely new opportunity (UNSW enterprise environmental monitoring EOI) and two new buyer-originated sources (Power & Water NT opportunities page; UNSW E-Tendering portal). As in recent runs, direct page fetching was unavailable (every external portal returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool), so detail is limited to publicly-searchable headline level and no corporate RFx could be read behind registration walls.
gaps: Direct page retrieval (WebFetch) returned HTTP 403 for every external site this run — including tenders.gov.au ATM index and detail pages, buy.nsw, Tenders WA/SA/Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders, Sydney Water, Rio Tinto Buy Local and Inner West Council. Tender detail pages and specification documents could not be opened, so deliverables/requirements extraction was not possible; findings rely on public search snippets only.

AusTender (tenders.gov.au) ATM list and individual ATM detail pages were reachable only via the search index, not direct fetch; specific publish/close dates for individual current ATMs could not be confirmed from the notice itself.

Tier-3 corporate opportunities (SAP Ariba Discovery, BHP Find Opportunities, Rio Tinto Ariba/Buy Local, NBN Co via Ariba, Fortescue) are login/registration-gated; no publicly-visible corporate RFx was found. Portals are recorded in discovered_sources with access_method=portal-registration; no corporate tenders were fabricated.
Invitation-only / panel-restricted RFx (e.g. management-consultancy RFPs) are out of scope and were not sought.

Web search index appears to lag by roughly 1-2 weeks: several returned listings had closing dates already in late June / early-to-mid July 2026 (already closed relative to the run date) and were excluded rather than recorded as open.

Power and Water Corporation (NT) QTOL showed a tender closing 23/07/2026 14:00 ACST, but the title and reference could not be resolved without portal access; the portal is recorded as a new source but the individual tender was not captured (no fabrication).
UNSW EOI closing date (2026-07-27) is taken from aggregated search listings; the buyer's own detail page sits behind the UNSW TenderLink login and could not be independently verified this run.
recommended_seed_additions: Power and Water Corporation (NT) — Opportunities to tender (https://www.powerwater.com.au/about/working-with-us/contractors-and-suppliers/opportunities-to-tender) — GBE, NT, buyer-originated.
University of New South Wales — E-Tendering portal via TenderLink (https://portal.tenderlink.com/unsw/) — university, NSW, free-registration buyer portal.

discovered_sources.source_name: Transport for NSW — Tenders and contracts

discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated public-health procurement body; distinct from the NSW Health tenders landing page already tracked. Individual opportunities run via the NSW Health Gateway / eTendering.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for NSW council current tenders; council publishes its own current-tenders register.
discovered_sources.notes: Local-government buyer-originated tender register.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for VendorPanel/council local-government tenders; Central Coast Council publishes its own tenders page.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Source-discovery search on new Australian government supplier portals surfaced reporting that the Commonwealth is rolling out a new AusTender Supplier Portal from mid-2026 - a publicly searchable database where suppliers self-register capabilities and credentials at no cost, which agencies browse when planning procurements (complements published ATMs).
discovered_sources.notes: New procurement-modernisation channel distinct from the existing GovPanels supplier portal. Registration opens from July 2026 per secondary reporting; not yet directly verifiable this run due to the egress block. Track for go-live and confirm the live URL/registration path on tenders.gov.au.

Public listing visible; full detail and lodgement via VendorPanel. New to this routine.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Surfaced while searching for Commonwealth 'become a supplier' / GBE procurement portals; a new Australian Government Supplier Portal is launching mid-2026 with supplier registration opening from July 2026 as part of the federal procurement modernisation agenda.

Currently a capability/registration directory rather than a tender board; worth monitoring as agencies begin sourcing capability through it. Complements AusTender. Origin not fetched this run (Tier-1 gov.au domains WAF-blocked).

discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated Victorian development agency running major works EOIs/RFPs. Not previously in the known-source list. Origin returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run; individual opportunity freshness (e.g. Games-related works) could not be confirmed and was not captured as a tender.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Appeared in a search for Australian council/eProcurement platforms. Procurement Australia is a national group-buying / aggregated-demand organisation that runs its own public RFTs (e.g. road signs & lighting, PPE/workwear, insurance broking & risk services) on behalf of member councils and agencies - i.e. buyer-originated collective procurement, not a paid reseller re-publishing others' notices. Free supplier registration; download docs and lodge responses via a secure eTender box.
discovered_sources.notes: New to this routine. Publishes on the TenderLink platform but Procurement Australia is itself the contracting/aggregating buyer, so it qualifies as buyer-originated. Public tender list at portal.tenderlink.com/procurementaustralia.
